title: Optimizing Neurorobot Policy under Limited Demonstration Data through Preference Regret
abstract: Robot reinforcement learning from demonstrations (RLfD) assumes that expert data is abundant; this is usually unrealistic in the real world given data scarcity as well as high collection cost. Furthermore, imitation learning algorithms assume that the data is independently and identically distributed, which ultimately results in poorer performance as gradual errors emerge and compound within test-time trajectories. We address these issues by introducing the "master your own expertise" (MYOE) framework, a self-imitation framework that enables robotic agents to learn complex behaviors from limited demonstration data samples. Inspired by human perception and action, we propose and design what we call the queryable mixture-of-preferences state space model (QMoP-SSM), which estimates the desired goal at every time step. These desired goals are used in computing the "preference regret", which is used to optimize the robot control policy. Our experiments demonstrate the robustness, adaptability, and out-of-sample performance of our agent compared to other state-of-the-art RLfD schemes. The GitHub repository that supports this work can be found at: https://github.com/rxng8/neurorobot-preference-regret-learning.
summary:
# 论文总结：Optimizing Neurorobot Policy under Limited Demonstration Data through Preference Regret

## 1. 核心创新点

这篇论文的核心是把有限示范下的 RLfD 从“尽量复制专家数据”改成“让机器人逐步 master your own expertise”：QMoP-SSM 在每个时刻估计偏好目标，再用 preference regret 作为优化信号，缓解少量 demonstration 导致的误差累积和 out-of-sample 性能退化。

## 2. 技术创新拆解

- **MYOE 自我模仿框架**：Master Your Own Expertise 让 agent 从有限示范出发，通过自身轨迹和偏好目标持续修正策略，而不是假设专家数据充足且 i.i.d.。
- **QMoP-SSM 偏好状态模型**：Queryable mixture-of-preferences state space model 用于估计每个时间步的 desired goal，把目标推断显式接入控制策略优化。
- **Preference regret 优化**：把实际行为与偏好目标之间的 regret 转化为学习信号，用来提升 limited demonstrations 下的鲁棒性、适应性和 out-of-sample 表现。

## 3. 方法论突破

- **新颖性**：与传统 RLfD 依赖大量专家示范或直接行为克隆不同，它引入偏好状态模型和 regret 优化，把示范不足问题转成可查询目标与自我改进问题。
- **关键技术**：核心技术是 MYOE self-imitation、QMoP-SSM desired-goal estimation、preference regret loss，以及有限示范数据下的策略更新流程。
- **理论支撑**：公开材料没有给出完整理论保证；它的贡献主要是神经机器人控制中的目标建模和偏好 regret 优化。

## 4. 实验验证

- **主要基准/场景**：ResearchTrend 页面显示论文包含 5 张图、4 张表和 13 页 appendix，并提供 GitHub 仓库；任务定位是 limited demonstration data 下的 neurorobot policy learning。
- **性能提升**：公开摘要报告相对其他 RLfD 方案具备更好的 robustness、adaptability 和 out-of-sample performance；具体数值需要读取论文表格。
- **消融实验**：公开摘要层面无法拆分 MYOE、QMoP-SSM 与 preference regret 的独立贡献；但从方法结构看，QMoP-SSM 提供目标估计，preference regret 提供直接优化信号。

## 5. 局限与启发

- **当前局限**：当前公开摘要没有给出具体任务名称、成功率或误差数值；方法也依赖偏好目标模型质量，若 QMoP-SSM 估计偏差大，regret 信号可能误导策略。
- **未来方向**：后续应补充跨机器人平台、不同 demonstration 数量、偏好模型失配和真实硬件部署的消融。
- **可迁移性**：对少示范、目标随时间变化且误差会累积的机器人学习任务有启发；对高维视觉操作或开放环境，需要更强的状态估计和偏好建模。

## 6. 一句话总结

它的核心价值是把少示范 RLfD 里的误差累积问题，改写成“偏好目标估计 + preference regret 优化”的自我改进问题。

recognized_fields.abstract: Robot reinforcement learning from demonstrations (RLfD) assumes that expert data is abundant; this is usually unrealistic in the real world given data scarcity as well as high collection cost. Furthermore, imitation learning algorithms assume that the data is independently and identically distributed, which ultimately results in poorer performance as gradual errors emerge and compound within test-time trajectories. We address these issues by introducing the "master your own expertise" (MYOE) framework, a self-imitation framework that enables robotic agents to learn complex behaviors from limited demonstration data samples. Inspired by human perception and action, we propose and design what we call the queryable mixture-of-preferences state space model (QMoP-SSM), which estimates the desired goal at every time step. These desired goals are used in computing the "preference regret", which is used to optimize the robot control policy. Our experiments demonstrate the robustness, adaptability, and out-of-sample performance of our agent compared to other state-of-the-art RLfD schemes.
